mapping.a_element: Hopf algebra symmetries in braided quantum field theories
mapping.b_element: renormalization group symmetries and universality in lattice quantum gravity

mapping.a_element: non-perturbative relations among correlation functions due to symmetries
mapping.b_element: non-perturbative lattice results connecting to continuum renormalization group

mapping.a_element: algebraic conditions for symmetries to hold in braided QFTs
mapping.b_element: renormalization group fixed points and scaling relations in lattice gravity

brief_justification: While both abstracts address non-perturbative aspects of quantum field theories and symmetries, paper A focuses on algebraic (Hopf algebra) symmetries in braided QFTs, whereas paper B centers on the renormalization group and lattice methods for quantum gravity. The underlying machinery—algebraic symmetry conditions vs. lattice-based renormalization group flow—differs fundamentally, despite superficial overlap in non-perturbative language.
